what_it_does: Defines an SVG icon for a checked checkbox: a rounded filled square using currentColor with a white checkmark path.
observations: Root SVG sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
The checkbox body is a rect at x=4 y=4 with width and height 16, rx=2, and fill=currentColor.
The checkmark is a path from 8,12 to 11,15 to 16,9 with stroke #FFFFFF.
Stroke settings use width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
standouts: The square inherits the surrounding text or CSS color through currentColor.
The checkmark color is fixed to white.
The icon is a compact single-line SVG asset.
conventions: Uses an SVG viewBox for scalable icon rendering.
Uses currentColor for theme-aware icon coloring on the checkbox body.
Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for the checkmark styling.
Mixes inherited color for the box with a hard-coded color for the checkmark.
concerns: The hard-coded white checkmark limits full single-color theming.
No title, role, or aria attributes are present in the SVG itself.
notes: Brief this as a theme-colored checked checkbox SVG with a fixed white tick.
questions: Should accessibility metadata be supplied by the embedding context?
Is the white checkmark required for all themes?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a checked checkbox icon, and the SVG renders a checked checkbox icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable checked-checkbox icon whose box can inherit UI color while preserving a white checkmark for contrast.
contribution: Contributes a checked-checkbox visual asset with a currentColor rounded square and explicit white checkmark that a fused system could keep as a form-state icon substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 SVG checked-checkbox icon with a currentColor rounded square and a white checkmark path.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing the checked state of a checkbox in an icon set or design system asset library.
